test_first_gate.evidence: Before the supervisor fix, module-only delivery while Bash waited on a real foreground helper exceeded the bounded cleanup timeout.
The 18-row matrix and launch, escalation, cleanup-failure, trap, monitor-mode, and rendezvous regressions passed after the fix.

test_first_gate.command: temporary harness mutation: replace process-group escalation with direct-PID signaling
test_first_gate.evidence: The process-survivor assertion detected the deliberately orphaned descendant.
Clean pre-checkpoint HEAD: 9432 passed, 0 failed, no skipped checks; post-checkpoint HEAD: 9489 passed, 0 failed.

sweep_evidence.2.3.0: Reconciled both _devflow_supervise_module callers and every scratch, supervisor PID, worker PID, cleanup, and signal-forwarding consumer.
sweep_evidence.2.3.0a: Checked the co-equal focused and full-suite boundaries; both use the same supervisor, pending-signal handshake, bounded group termination, and scratch cleanup contracts.
sweep_evidence.2.3.0b: SignalBoundary, SignalName, and SignalDeliveryScope literals exactly match the 2x3x3 matrix; no unhandled value remains.
sweep_evidence.2.3.0c: Traced TMPDIR to validated scratch leaf, parent to supervisor PID rendezvous, supervisor to worker/group signaling, and cleanup return codes to boundary outcomes.
sweep_evidence.2.3.1: Reviewed deleted inline cleanup, direct wait, and positional tuple setup; replacement ownership and named-state paths are live and tested.
sweep_evidence.2.3.2: No method, file, route, or page was deleted; no stranded dependent found.
sweep_evidence.2.3.3: ruff, bash -n, warning-level shellcheck, and git diff --check passed on touched code.
sweep_evidence.2.3.4: Verified POSIX capability gating and official Python API availability; negative cleanup, launch, missing-rendezvous, and signal-resistant boundary rows pass.
sweep_evidence.2.3.4a: Identifier-scoped comment-analyzer re-sweep found two wording overclaims; both were corrected before commit.
sweep_evidence.2.3.5: Shared supervisor and group-termination helpers remove duplicated focused/full-suite control flow; no simpler contract-preserving path found.
sweep_evidence.2.3.6: Cleanup return codes are folded into non-green outcomes; bounded waits, pending launch signals, and finally-group cleanup close silent failure paths.
sweep_evidence.2.3.7: The exact matrix cardinality is asserted as 18 unique rows and result/failure tallies are verified without ordering assumptions.

phase3_findings.description: The full-suite process-group comment contradicted the distinct module group.
The focused-runner process-group comment contradicted the distinct module group.
Module-only delivery could hang behind a real foreground helper.
The module signal trap was deferred behind a real foreground helper.
The in-shell fixture did not model deferred traps behind an external child.
Timeout cleanup omitted the distinct module process group.
POSIX-only signal APIs were unconditional on native Windows.
The matrix lacked capability-aware native-Windows handling.
The release note promised reliable cleanup while hangs and races remained.
The full-suite boundary could lose a signal before capturing the child PID.
The focused boundary had the same post-launch PID-capture race.
Signal forwarding used an unbounded wait for a resistant group.
The module duplicate-signal comment was impossible under the group topology.
Normal full-suite paths discarded tally cleanup failures.
Module cleanup marked completion before successful removal.
The positional fixture tuple permitted silent Path-role swaps.
Open primitive matrix dimensions permitted invalid fallback routing.
The tests did not exercise the pre-PID-capture launch window.
No resistant-helper row proved bounded escalation and reaping.
Rows did not assert runner exit or full-suite failure semantics.
No removal-failure injection proved a loud non-green outcome.
Trap restoration did not verify caller job-control mode preservation.
Caller traps were restored while the boundary still owned its tally.

fix_decisions.evidence: A self-contradicting changed claim is non-demotable and would misdirect maintenance of signal ownership.
A self-contradicting changed claim is non-demotable and would misdirect maintenance of signal ownership.
The observable failure was an unbounded run with descendant and scratch leakage.
The observable failure was an unbounded run with descendant and scratch leakage.
The missing fixture allowed the core bounded-cleanup guarantee to pass while the real foreground-child path hung.
Failure cleanup could strand a distinct module group, but only on an already failing/timeout test path.
Official API availability confirms collection would fail on native Windows instead of skipping by capability.
Unsupported-host collection failure is material but does not affect POSIX execution semantics.
The branch's customer-facing claim directly contradicted reproduced hangs and races.
A signal in the window could terminate the boundary while leaving the just-launched module group orphaned.
A signal in the window could terminate the runner while leaving the just-launched module group orphaned.
A resistant descendant could block interruption forever; bounded escalation is required by the acceptance contract.
The changed comment contradicted the process topology and could prompt incorrect signal handling changes.
The run could report green while leaving a private artifact; the failure is bounded and diagnostic once propagated.
A removal failure could be silently laundered into success and suppress later cleanup retry.
Without deterministic coverage, the orphan-producing race could remain while the core matrix stayed green.
Without a resistant helper, the bounded-exit guarantee was unproven and the old unbounded wait passed.
Cleanup-only assertions could miss wrong exit/failure-tally semantics even when artifacts disappeared.
The prior suite could not prove cleanup errors affected the verdict, allowing silent green leaks.
Leaking job-control mode into callers changes shell behavior after the helper returns.
A narrow signal window could bypass private-tally cleanup and leave an artifact; the run otherwise remained bounded.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Forced escalation leaves scratch roots owned by selectable modules that do not consume DEVFLOW_MODULE_OWNED_SCRATCH_ROOT.
fix_decisions.evidence: A reproduced parent-only TERM left devflow-raf-contract.* after the worker was killed, directly violating the no-surviving-scratch acceptance contract.

fix_decisions.claim_text: The POSIX capability skip is not propagated into the suite skip tally.
fix_decisions.evidence: A fully skipped unittest class exits zero and run.sh records only PASS, so unsupported-host coverage loss is invisible in the terminal tally.

fix_decisions.claim_text: The launch-window test hook waits forever without an external release after a pending signal.
fix_decisions.evidence: The unbounded path is gated entirely behind DEVFLOW_TEST_LAUNCH_WINDOW_FILE and cannot affect production runs, but it weakens deterministic test instrumentation.

fix_decisions.claim_text: A scratch cleanup failure is reported as a module process exit status.
fix_decisions.evidence: The changed recap factually contradicts the observed successful module exit; self-contradicting changed diagnostics are non-demotable.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Nested job control can launch the worker outside the supervisor process group.
fix_decisions.evidence: A PTY probe observed distinct supervisor and worker PGIDs, so forwarding and escalation can miss the worker on a supported execution topology.

fix_delta_gate.evidence: Blinded reviewer verified guard/consumer contracts, launch races, bounded escalation and reaping, cleanup/error propagation, POSIX assumptions, trap restoration, and matrix fidelity.

phase3_findings.description: Forced escalation removes only the boundary root while other selectable modules allocate sibling scratch roots directly under TMPDIR; a review-and-fix reproduction left devflow-raf-contract.* behind.
The POSIX-only unittest class can skip every signal row and exit zero while run.sh discards successful output and records no host-capability skip.
The test-only launch-window hook busy-spins until an external .release file even after the runner has recorded a pending signal.
Focused and full-suite validation failures clear the newly allocated scratch path before cleanup, orphaning a relative-TMPDIR allocation.
Focused scratch cleanup failure overwrites MODULE_RC and falsely reports that a successful module process exited with status 1.
The supervisor launches its worker while nested monitor mode is enabled, which can put the worker in another PGID under a PTY and outside forwarded group signals.
SignalRowState remains a positional aggregate that permits boundary and Path-role mismatches despite being frozen.
PID readers accept zero, so a corrupted test PID can turn group termination into kill -0 against the caller's own process group.

phase2_fails.description: Validation failure loses the cleanup handle, and create-issue self-allocation lacks equivalent path validation before recursive cleanup.
The general runner fixture does not scrub every newly consumed test hook, and resistant-helper coverage omits the parent-boundary path.
The release note omits the POSIX process-group capability scope enforced by the matrix.

devflow_reflection: early shadow review completed with full six-reviewer coverage and promoted 11 new finding/checklist records to iteration 2
over-grade calibration gate: S-1 Important, S-2 Important, S-3 calibrated to Suggestion, S-5 Critical, and S-6 Important recorded with technical evidence
